layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN FINANCIAL DATA LAYOUT with equal-width side-by-side tables. The slide uses a left-to-right orientation with two major content regions of equal weight. Both tables are RECREATE containers and will be rebuilt using Section 8 Option A (Standard Financial Table) design patterns. The layout maximizes horizontal space utilization by allocating roughly 48% width to each table with a 4% gutter between them. Vertical space is fully utilized — tables expand to fill all available height between the title area and slide bottom margin. No outer content container is used; tables sit directly on the slide background. No decorative bottom layer is needed because the two tables together provide complete slide coverage. The design follows a formal financial data aesthetic: merged header rows with brand primary fill, light grey sub-headers, white data rows with dashed grey horizontal separators, and no vertical cell borders. All alignment is precise — both tables share identical top and bottom edges (horizontal alignment rule B2). The layout supports natural left-to-right reading flow, with the left table (Bookings) presenting input metrics and the right table (Revenues) presenting output metrics.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.48, 0.48]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT TABLE — Bookings 2026): Left column, equal-peers with RIGHT TABLE, split 1:1 horizontally. This container holds a multi-region financial table showing Q1 bookings by region (NA and Intl), customer, and country, with monthly breakdowns (Jan, Feb, Mar). The table has approximately 18 data rows plus headers. DESIGN INTENT: Rebuild as a native PowerPoint table using Section 8 Option A (Standard Financial Table). Header row: merged across all columns, solid brand primary fill, white bold text left-aligned and vertically centered, no top/bottom border, text reads 'Bookings - 2026 (Net)'. Sub-header row: column labels (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar), bold text, light brand fill (tinted version of primary color), left-aligned for text columns and right-aligned for numeric columns. Data rows: white background, all vertical cell borders removed, horizontal borders between rows are thin dashed grey lines. Region column cells (NA, Intl): merged for grouped rows, light grey background, bold text, vertically centered and left-aligned. Customer and Country columns: left-aligned, regular weight. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar): right-aligned, regular weight. Subtotal rows ('Total NA', 'Total Intl', 'Total'): bold text, light grey background. Highlighted cells: preserve the light green fill color exactly as shown in the source for cells with values (e.g., Jan column for U Chicago, McGill, Albert Einstein, New Intl - 2026 DACH). Column widths: Region column narrow (just enough for 'Region' label), Customer column sized to fit longest name without wrapping, Country column narrow (just enough for two-letter codes), numeric columns share remaining space equally. Table expands vertically to fill all available space between title bottom margin and slide bottom margin. Font: theme body font throughout, medium size for readability given the row count. SPECIAL NOTES: Top and bottom edges must align exactly with RIGHT TABLE (C1). The table must fit all 18+ rows without clipping — if necessary, reduce row padding and use dashed separators to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space (Beauty Principle 0.1 and 0.10).
  C1 (RIGHT TABLE — Revenues 2026): Right column, equal-peers with LEFT TABLE, split 1:1 horizontally. This container holds a multi-region financial table showing Q1 revenues by region (NA and Intl), customer, and country, with monthly breakdowns (Jan, Feb, Mar). The table has approximately 16 data rows plus headers. DESIGN INTENT: Rebuild as a native PowerPoint table using Section 8 Option A (Standard Financial Table). Header row: merged across all columns, solid brand primary fill, white bold text left-aligned and vertically centered, no top/bottom border, text reads 'Revenues - 2026'. Sub-header row: column labels (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar), bold text, light brand fill (tinted version of primary color), left-aligned for text columns and right-aligned for numeric columns. Data rows: white background, all vertical cell borders removed, horizontal borders between rows are thin dashed grey lines. Region column cells (NA, Intl): merged for grouped rows, light grey background, bold text, vertically centered and left-aligned. Customer and Country columns: left-aligned, regular weight. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar): right-aligned, regular weight. Subtotal rows ('Total NA', 'Total Intl', 'Total'): bold text, light grey background. Highlighted cells: preserve the light green fill color exactly as shown in the source for cells with values (e.g., Jan column for U Chicago, Feb column for McGill, Mar column for Albert Einstein). Column widths: Region column narrow (just enough for 'Region' label), Customer column sized to fit longest name without wrapping, Country column narrow (just enough for two-letter codes or 'Intl'), numeric columns share remaining space equally. Table expands vertically to fill all available space between title bottom margin and slide bottom margin. Font: theme body font throughout, medium size for readability given the row count. SPECIAL NOTES: Top and bottom edges must align exactly with LEFT TABLE (C0). The table must fit all 16+ rows without clipping — if necessary, reduce row padding and use dashed separators to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space (Beauty Principle 0.1 and 0.10). The right table has fewer rows than the left table, so row heights should be proportionally larger to maintain identical top and bottom alignment across both tables.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BOTTOM LAYER): NOT ACTIVE. The two tables together provide complete slide coverage and visual structure. No bottom decorative layer is needed (Rule D1 — only use when content does not fill the slide or when visual grouping is required).
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): NOT ACTIVE. No cross-container connectors or overlay elements are needed for this slide. The two tables are independent data presentations with no visual links between them.
